summary: Small TY subsystem matching-improvement slice split from PR #2617. The loaded diff changes local variable ordering/use in `tydisplay.c` and field/access expressions in `tylist.c`; decomp-dev reported two unmatched-item improvements: `fn_80313BD8` from 97.03% to 98.40% and `un_8031BC54` from 99.51% to 99.73%. No human review comments were present in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Adjusted `un_8031BC54` local layout and argument reuse by introducing `s32 id = arg0`, moving `TyDspArchNames` declarations later, and passing `id` to helper calls.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported `main/melee/ty/tydisplay` `un_8031BC54` +2 bytes, 99.51% to 99.73%

key_files.role: Adjusted `fn_80313BD8` access expressions for TY list state fields, replacing `jobj`, `entries[...]`, and temporary `idx` use with offset-style fields such as `x288`, `x274`, `x270`, and `x278`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `fn_80313BD8` changes from `state->jobj` to `state->x288`, `entries[0]` to `x274`/`x270`, and `entries[10].links[0]` to `x278`
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported `main/melee/ty/tylist` `fn_80313BD8` +27 bytes, 97.03% to 98.40%

what_changed.description: The PR is a focused `src/melee/ty` slice split from #2617 for reviewability.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `Split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so the matching improvements are easier to review.`

what_changed.description: `un_8031BC54` now keeps `arg0` in a local `s32 id`, uses that local for `un_8031B9DC` and `un_8031BF34`, and places `TyDspArchNames` locals after `data`.
`fn_80313BD8` now uses TY state offset-style members for JObj/list entry pointers and removes a short-lived `s16 idx` temporary in one branch.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `state->entries[0]` changed to `state->x274` and `state->x270` in different branches
raw_diff: removed `s16 idx;` and direct `state->x270->idx` use

human_pr_text: preliminary report says the full unsplit source branch had no broken matches/regressions in the current post-rebase report

smart_moves.description: Kept the PR narrow to the TY directory so matching improvements could be reviewed independently from the larger #2617 branch.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: split by directory/subsystem for easier review

smart_moves.description: Used small codegen-sensitive edits rather than broad rewrites: local declaration ordering/argument copy in `tydisplay.c` and targeted state-access substitutions in `tylist.c`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: only two changed files, small hunks
changed_files.jsonl: total loaded changed-file metadata is +16/-18 across two files

smart_moves.description: Included local check context and deferred authoritative scoring to per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `Per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting on this draft is the authoritative slice score once it completes.`

decomp_lessons.lesson: For near-matching functions, local declaration order and preserving an argument in an explicit local can be meaningful MWCC codegen levers, but this PR only supports treating them as narrow matching tactics backed by score evidence, not as general style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `s32 id = arg0` added and used for helper calls

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered struct's semantic fields do not match codegen, direct offset-style fields may remain useful as interim matching evidence; do not over-promote semantic field names without enough type evidence.

naming_conventions.convention: The PR uses address-style function names and offset-style struct member names where semantic names are not established or not retained by the matching attempt.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: changed TY state member accesses
raw_diff: unchanged function labels in diff headers

naming_conventions.convention: A short local `id` was introduced for an integer argument reused as an identifier-like parameter in `un_8031BC54`; the PR evidence supports the local's codegen role but not a broader naming rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce a same-value local copy of a function parameter and use it at callsites to influence register/lifetime shape.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported `un_8031BC54` +2 bytes improvement

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Tune local declaration order for stack/register layout in a near-matching function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `TyDspArchNames jobj_names` and `matanim_names` declarations moved below `data`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use more specific offset-style state members instead of array/member expressions when the latter produces worse codegen.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reported `fn_80313BD8` +27 bytes improvement

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Remove a short-lived local temporary when direct field access gives better codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: else branch now calls `un_80313358(state, (s8) state->x270->idx, 4, 1)`

accepted_candidate_records.title: Explicit parameter local and declaration ordering can move near-match scores
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2629, `un_8031BC54` improved after introducing `s32 id = arg0`, using `id` at helper calls, and moving `TyDspArchNames` declarations below other locals. Treat as narrow MWCC codegen evidence, not broad style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Offset-style TY state fields were used as interim matching evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2629, `fn_80313BD8` improved after replacing semantic-looking `jobj`/`entries[...]` accesses with offset-style fields including `x288`, `x274`, `x270`, and `x278`. Do not infer those names are final semantic fields from this PR alone.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2629 as an example under matching tactics requiring evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2629 is a compact example where declaration order, an explicit argument local, and offset-style field accesses improved unmatched-item scores, but the evidence is score improvement rather than full matching. If documented, present it as a narrow tactic requiring objdiff/decomp-dev evidence and later cleanup when type information is recovered.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report with two unmatched-item improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR illustrates evidence-backed local codegen steering and the risk of treating offset-style fields as final semantics.

rejection_notes: Do not record `toy.c` as a changed file from the loaded slice; it appears only in the PR body file list, not in changed_files metadata or the loaded diff.
Do not claim either function fully matched; the loaded bot report only says they improved in unmatched items.
Do not infer human reviewer preferences or requested changes; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not generalize the offset-style `x###` fields as preferred source style; the evidence only shows they improved this function's score in this slice.
